core_payload.content:
# N-Infinity Swarm Roster

## 1. Boundary
N-Infinity Swarm Roster owns the named worker roles that make up the active N-Infinity family. This sub-hub exists so the swarm's role map can be navigated as a coherent roster instead of remaining mixed into substrate, signal, and governance detail on the parent hub.

## 2. What Belongs Here
This sub-hub groups `n-infinity.weaver`, `n-infinity.gardener`, `n-infinity.parliament`, `n-infinity.innovator`, `n-infinity.reminder-agent`, `n-infinity.risk-detector`, and `n-infinity.suggestion-agent`. Together they define the resonance, hygiene, contradiction, synthesis, reminder, risk, and suggestion roles that give the swarm differentiated behavior.

## 3. What Does Not Belong Here
This sub-hub does not own the runtime substrate that hosts the swarm, the external lanes that feed it, or the governance metrics that evaluate its state. Those belong to `runtime-fabric`, `signal-fabric`, and `governance-metrics`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: N-Infinity Swarm Roster groups the named worker roles of the swarm: Weaver, Gardener, Parliament, Innovator, Reminder, Risk Detector, and Suggestion Agent. It keeps the role map explicit and separate from substrate, input signals, and governance metrics. This matters because the active worker family should remain navigable as a coherent roster, with each role understood as part of one bounded swarm surface rather than dissolved into runtime mechanics, external inputs, or abstract governance structures.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted as part of n-infinity hub decomposition to reduce parent link density from 26 outbound to ≤9.
